programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: In Herefordshire, Jules Hudson discovers why a good dose of country air might not be all that it's cracked up to be. John Craven enjoys the breathtaking north Cornwall coast, while Paul Martin is in the Brecon Beacons helping on a mission to save Britain's most endangered mammal - the water vole(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts looks over two houses - one in Wimbledon and another in Birmingham - and Dion Dublin meets the buyers of a semi-detached in Bootle, Merseyside(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The disturbing trade in counterfeit airbags that will not work in the event of a crash, and a series of dawn raids are carried out to crack down on rogue landlords(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A base jumper crash lands on a rocky beach and a mum relives the moment her young daughter fell from her pony at a competition and was trampled underfoot(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the teams are joined by trusted experts Raj Bisram and Catherine Southon, as they go Bargain Hunting in Kingston upon Thames(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara and Becky's night out takes an interesting turn, while Ruhma visits Laura and Russell with some gifts. Al gets dragged into 'a situation' with a patient that he would rather have avoided(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits the Isle of Arran to house-hunt with a novelist and her husband, who are in search of writing inspiration from the Scottish wilderness for their £375,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brothers help Elizabeth from Brighton to turn a paved-over blank square of land into a city garden full of colour and plants, on a healthy budget of £6,000(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich visit Mansfield to help Annette, who moved into her home with her three sons two years ago and is now ready to renovate the garden(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As a gunshot echoes round Albert Square, events inside the Vic go from bad to worse as someone's life hangs in the balance. Bianca tries to talk to Tiffany about everything that happened with Evie(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Four celebrities try to book their place in the semi-final, cooking a two-course meal to be judged by former champions Angellica Bell and Phil Vickery, and 2015 finalist Rylan Clark-Neil. With their last chance to impress, emotions in the kitchen are running high and the pressure proves too much for some contestants. The celebrity judges deliver some frank and cutting responses to some of the dishes, but others fare much better(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Derren Litten's comedy following a motley band of Scarborough residents who are bonded by family, friendship and their love of karaoke. It opens a week after a devastated Karen caught Mike drunkenly kissing another woman at the back of a seafront crab stall. It's only when they bump into each other at their regular Friday karaoke night that Mike finally has a chance to explain. But will Karen forgive him? Starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites Kaiser Chiefs' frontman Ricky Wilson, and comedians Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons to express their anger over people and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include new towels, escalators and the bartender at the local pub(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Italy. John Inverdale presents action from both teams' final match before the World Cup, which came from St James' Park. With commentary by Andrew Cotter and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. Phil Davis, Niamh Cusack and Tom Georgeson guest star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Bird and Claire Thomson, and special guest Phyllis Logan. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary joins the crowds as she attends a music festival for the first time to explore the pop-up food stalls and enlists headliner Rick Astley to help make her speedy cheesy muffins. She also prepares scrumptious rosemary lamb steaks, vegetable kebabs and rum and raisin ice-cream(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including the Euro 2020 qualifiers taking place over the course of the week(n)

programme.title: Transplant Games
programme.desc: Alan Shearer tells the story of athletes whose lives were transformed by transplant surgery(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage from Stockton-on-Tees, as elite athletes compete in both track and field events. Double Paralympic gold medallist Jonnie Peacock is set to take part in the to take part in the IPC 100m, and heptathlon star Katarina Johnson-Thompson plans to double up in the long jump and 150m(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Memorial Van Damme. Highlights from the final Diamond League meeting of the year, with some of the world's best competing at the King Baudouin Stadium. This was the second part of the grand final, following last weekend's opener in Zurich. As well as the overall Diamond League champions being crowned, the winner of each event on the night would get a wildcard entry to the World Championships, which start at the end of this month. Among the stars expected to be in action were Great Britain's 100m gold medal hope in Doha, Dina Asher-Smith, American sprint sensation Noah Lyles in the 200m and reigning Olympic 110m hurdle champion Omar McLeod of Jamaica(n)

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: Game show in which two pairs of complete strangers team up, each battling to outdo the other by listing as much as they know about a given subject. The first couple to win two rounds get to play for £50,000 and each time the champions defeat a new set of opponents their winnings mount up(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, the contestants are Rick Edwards, Michael Brooks, Mark Thompson, Maggie Aderin- Pocock, Heather Couper, Nigel Henbest, Dallas Campbell and Lucie Green(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The pro-celebrity ballroom contest hosted by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman returns with a bang, as the Queen of Pop herself Kylie Minogue performs a medley of her biggest hits alongside a dazzling routine from the professionals. The line-up this year features former England footballers Alex Scott and David James, TV presenter Anneka Rice, actresses Catherine Tyldesley and Emma Barton, stand-up comedian Chris Ramsey, Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin, fashion model Emma Weymouth, Olympic gold medallist James Cracknell, Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing, CBBC presenter Karim Zeroual, singer Michelle Visage, BBC Breakfast sports correspondent Mike Bushell, YouTuber Saffron Barker and Paralympic table tennis player Will Bayley. Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood all return to the judging desk, joined by the newest member of the panel Motsi Mabuse, who replaces the outgoing Darcey Bussell. The show also sees the celebs take to the floor for the very first time in a spectacular group d(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Archie takes matters into her own hands to try and get justice for a wronged patient, and decides to help him escape from the emergency department before the police can catch him. Already making progress with his mental health after his suicide attempt, Iain runs into an old friend, Dixie, and their chance meeting encourages Iain to think about his potential and his future. Meanwhile, Rash struggles to cope under an increasing amount of pressure, and David finds it difficult living with his teenage son(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as a doting mother-of-six who constantly interferes in her children's lives. Agnes tries to solve her son Dermot's relationship problems by giving his girlfriend advice, and Grandad ends up in hospital after Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes awry(n)

programme.title: Sliding Doors (1998)
programme.desc: A London woman's entire future hinges on whether or not she catches a train. In one version of events, she gets the chance to start afresh and lead a happy new life, but in the other she is stuck supporting her lazy, unfaithful boyfriend. Comedy drama, presenting two parallel stories featuring the same character. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, John Hannah and John Lynch(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: The seven remaining contestants are tested on their lyric writing and their flow, as they are challenged to perform over a host of beats with different BPMs, from afrobeat to drum 'n' bass. Then, the artists are tasked with penning and performing a heartfelt dedication to a loved one(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's opening game of the season, Chicago Bears v Green Bay Packers at Soldier Field(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Victoria Fritz present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage of the world's biggest half-marathon, which sees tens of thousands of runners of all abilities negotiate the course from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields. British favourite Mo Farah won the men's elite race for a record fifth time in 2018, beating New Zealand's Jake Robertson into second place as he did 12 months earlier. A home favourite also prevailed in the men's wheelchair race, with David Weir claiming his seventh title here, while Kenyan Vivian Cheruiyot won the elite women's event for the second time in three years and Poland's Martyna Snopek was the women's wheelchair champion(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores sacred architecture 350 years after Christopher Wren started work on St Paul's Cathedral. Josie d'Arby meets Christian artists collective Morphe Arts(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an active couple return to their roots in Shropshire, looking for a property within their £350,000 budget that gives them the great outdoors on their doorstep(n)

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore reclaims three items from a Walsall recycling centre, with designer Rupert Blanchard given the task of bringing a boring bookcase into the 21st century(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kelly Cates, Adrian Chiles, Andy Hamilton, Kate Williams, Mark Dolan, Rustie Lee, Esther Rantzen and Tim Vine(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition marking 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy waits by the water at Charlie's Yard to meet a dangerous adversary, but despite their past grievances the pair agree to a truce. Tommy receives a message from a mysterious stranger during his constituency surgery, while at his Westminster office, the MP makes Aberama a very tempting offer, and later invites Mosley to a party he is throwing for Lizzie's birthday. On the night of the event, things soon take a turn for the worse when a surprise guest arrives. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cambourne, Cambridgeshire, Marcus and Rebecca ask the Rich Brothers and Arit Anderson for an Australia-meets-Scotland garden, but with a £2,500 budget, it may prove difficult(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel around Yorkshire, finding such rarities as a vintage set of weights and a Victorian skirt lifter, before an auction in Rotherham(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty advises on essential summer pruning and adds French beans to the vegetable garden. The programme also meets a gardener in Hertfordshire who combines her love of cottage garden plants with tropical plants and visits a garden in Kent where pots are the stars of the summer show(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates easy bakes that can be rustled up in no time, including banana tarte Tatin with speedy homemade ice-cream, a massive meatloaf Wellington, and an effortless fruity soda bread that slashes traditional baking time in half. On the road, Nadiya gains access to the Marmite factory in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, to find out how one of her favourite short-cut ingredients is produced, while in Keynsham, near Bristol, she reveals the secrets of an ingenious pasta bake to a new mother(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A British nurse races to rescue a critically ill baby in a remote town in the Bush, while a group of UK cops deal with trouble at a funeral in the Outback(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The death of David Ray reveals the separate lives of a father and son in the same house. Plus, an act of bravery is uncovered in the search for David Walker's heirs(n)

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Birmingham's factories were crucial to war production, and though the city was heavily bombed during the Second World War, much of the destruction was kept secret. Hollywood actor David Harewood takes an aerial journey over his home city to see the land from the perspective of the German pilots and talks to survivors on the ground about their recollections of the attacks(n)

programme.title: The Wooden Horse (1950)
programme.desc: British PoWs in a German camp try to escape their captors by taking up gymnastics, and using a vaulting horse to conceal the tunnel they are digging. However, even if they manage to escape the camp, the journey to neutral territory will not be an easy one. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Leo Genn, David Tomlinson, Anthony Steel and David Greene(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: The SSPCA team receives a delivery of a nest of orphaned red squirrel kits, while head vet Romain Pizzi uses an ancient falconer's trick to get an injured bird flying again after severe wing damage(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the challenges of restoring three cherished family heirlooms, bringing back to life a painting, a ceramic plaque and a Japanese lacquered jewellery box(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are The Islands of Great Britain, the ‘Pop Larkin Chronicles' by HE Bates, The League of Gentlemen and Doctor Who, followed by the general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost gives advice on how to prune overgrown evergreen shrubs and adds to his borders to create height. Rachel de Thame shows how to maintain containers and refresh with plants that will extend the display into autumn, and there's a feature on a gardener whose frequent absences from home have influenced the design and planting of his garden(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The rehearsals for Chicago are finally under way, having been delayed by Fosse's hospitalisation for a heart attack, but his insecurities soon come to the fore. Bob is hard on Gwen in rehearsals and suggests she is having problems keeping up with the younger cast, while she is concerned about the ending of the show and asks for a new song(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The long-running comedy quiz returns for another series, with Sandi Toksvig joined by panellists Loyiso Gola, Jason Manford, Sarah Millican and Alan Davies to answer questions about quirky topics. As ever, the panellists will not necessarily be expected to deliver the correct answers, and Sandi will be handing out points to those who provide the 'most interesting' guesses(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard presents the cultural review show. As anticipation mounts around the publication of David Cameron's autobiography, a panel discusses the art of the memoir(n)

programme.title: The Levelling (2016)
programme.desc: A trainee vet returns to her family farm after her brother's suicide and attempts to build bridges with her estranged father. Drama, starring Ellie Kendrick, David Troughton and Jack Holden(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on whether a crisis point in education funding has been reached, with cameras following staff and pupils at North Denes Primary in Great Yarmouth through a challenging summer term. To balance the books, the headteacher has to lose almost a fifth of her staff and cut services that help families in the wider community. Boris Johnson has pledged to boost school funding - but will it be enough, and will the extra money go to the schools that need it most?(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes his group of chefs to Bergen, Norway, where they are challenged to emulate the precise style of well-regarded local chef Christopher Haatuft's award-winning restaurant. The chefs get an in-depth course in the specifics of Scandinavian cooking and ingredients as they prepare venison stew. A more experienced chef joins the brigade to help them rise to the challenge of preparing food for an award-winning restaurant, leaving some of the existing members fearful that they will be replaced(n)

programme.title: The Day Mountbatten Died
programme.desc: On 27 August, 1979, Lord Louis Mountbatten, great uncle to Prince Charles, was blown up at sea by the IRA off the west coast of Ireland. Three others were killed on the boat that day, including two teenage boys, and later that afternoon the IRA killed 18 British soldiers across the border in Northern Ireland. Forty years on, this is the story of that remarkable Bank Holiday Monday told by those directly affected by it(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: Ali-A helps young photographer Oliver transform his family car both inside and out. However, what Oliver's parents will think of the car's unique new look remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Marnie struggles to accept her new position in the team(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Vim Kumaratunga and Lindsey Chapman(n)

programme.title: The Animal's Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham focuses on woodland creatures, using high-speed photography to capture the goshawk as it negotiates dense thicket. He also explores the lives of hedgehogs and fallow deer, finds out where stag beetles like to reside, and meets a Briton who is determined to make the nation a better home for the red squirrel(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain and Angela Scanlon present the third round, with robots Apex, Vulture, Terrorhurtz, Track-Tion, Bucky the Robot and Rapid battling it out. Among the contestants controlling the machines are a group of schoolchildren who are this series' youngest entrants and a team that has spent more money on their robot than any of their rivals(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Italy. John Inverdale introduces action from the World Cup warm-up fixture at St James' Park, as England aimed for a confidence-boosting send-off and sought to extend their 100 per cent winning record against Italy. Eddie Jones' side overwhelmed the Azzurri with an eight-try victory during this year's Six Nations, and have won all 25 meetings between the sides. Commentary comes from Andrew Cotter and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: Nigel's tour of the flavours of Britain takes him from a classic Lancashire hotpot to three melt-in-the-mouth one-pot dishes with origins from around the world. He meets Yasmin, whose fesenjoon takes her back to her childhood in Iran, gets a lesson in Brazil's national dish feijoada from brothers Andre and Anderson, and discovers the delicate flavours of a traditional Bangladeshi korma as he joins Enam and his family to celebrate Eid. Inspired by their dishes, Nigel creates a luxurious bean hotpot bejewelled with beetroot and carrot(n)

programme.title: The Battle of the River Plate (1956)
programme.desc: British naval forces pursue German battleship the Admiral Graf Spee, finally blockading the vessel in Montevideo harbour, where its commanding officer is faced with an impossible choice. Second World War adventure from Powell and Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Anthony Quayle, Peter Finch, Bernard Lee and Jack Gwillim(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team investigates the culture, history and legends of Britain and its people, beginning with the nation's islands. Nick Crane learns how Fair Isle's small community has survived and Neil Oliver visits Orkney to explore conspiracy theories surrounding the death of Lord Kitchener. Tessa Dunlop heads for the Western Isles to investigate an optical phenomenon known as the green ray, Andy Torbet climbs a chalk pinnacle on the Isle of Wight, and folk singer June Tabor performs a traditional ballad(n)

programme.title: Jungle Atlantis
programme.desc: A thousand years ago, the Khmer people of Cambodia built an empire and their capital was the great city of Angkor, with its centrepiece being Angkor Wat - a vast temple complex covering an area more than four times the size of the Vatican City. In the first of this two-part programme, an international team of archaeologists and scientists use revolutionary technology to reveal the true scale and extent of the lost metropolis and find out how its people lived and died(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Highlands, new entrant crofters Lynn and Sandra welcome more new sheep and take delivery of a batch of hens that they hope will lay the foundations of an 'egg empire'. In Northern Ireland, the Rodgers host a barbecue for the local Young Farmers Club, but it is no ordinary flame-grilled affair, while Raymond and Johnny gather the flock to wean this year's lambs in the Cairngorms(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A fishing boat in Salcombe goes up in flames with the skipper still aboard, while on the Isle of Sheppey, the lifeboat crew race to the aid of a man stuck in freezing mud in the middle of the night. A dog falls into the river Thames, creating an emergency for the Chiswick crew when the owner dives in to save it - and soon needs rescuing herself(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: It's the busiest time of year and the number of orphaned animals being admitted to the rescue centre has reached record levels. April is bottle-feeding roe deer fawn Petal, but needs to call on the help of Chloe and Nicola when she is also given the task of looking after newborn hedgehog Piglet(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents the magazine to accompany the BBC Proms, joined by saxophonist Jess Gillam, baritone Gerald Finley and composer Daniel Kidane, with a season finale ahead of the Last Night of the Proms next Saturday. Tom Service takes maestro Sakari Oramo for a musical chat in an Austin Maestro and Last Night soloist Jamie Barton sings out the show(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitler's ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: After having their headquarters bombed, the air-raid wardens have to share the church hall with the Home Guard, but Hodges' men are terribly disruptive and soon cause mayhem - especially when they decide to start a fire since the premises are a bit draughty. Comedy, first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and Bill Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Monty Python: Almost the Truth - The BBC Lawyer's Cut
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the comedy troupe Monty Python. Starting with their humble beginnings, the programme examines how the members met, their early influences and how their transition into films changed the face of comedy. Featuring contributions by John Cleese, Terry Jones, Terry Gilliam, Michael Palin and Eric Idle, along with archive interviews from Graham Chapman(n)

programme.title: Python at 50: Silly Talks and Holy Grails
programme.desc: A selection of rarely seen Monty Python moments from the BBC archives, following the group's encounters with 'Auntie' over the past 50 years and capturing them at the height of their power. Highlights include Terry Gilliam showing how he created his unique Python animations, Graham Chapman discussing the pressures of life as a homosexual, alcoholic comic, John Cleese explaining why he had to quit the final TV series, and Michael Palin promoting The Life of Brian on a children's programme(n)

programme.title: Monty Python's Flying Circus
programme.desc: Classic comedy sketch series, with John Cleese, Eric Idle, Michael Palin and the team. Picasso paints an abstract while riding a bicycle, and Ernest Scribbler writes the funniest joke in the world(n)

programme.title: In the Loop (2009)
programme.desc: A witless government minister makes a few ill-chosen remarks on the possibility of Britain and the US going to war in the Middle East, and suddenly finds himself out of his depth in international politics as pro and anti-war factions try to influence him and a fearsome spin doctor is sent to control the situation. Satirical comedy, starring Peter Capaldi, Tom Hollander and James Gandolfini(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Karachi (2013)
programme.desc: A Pakistani woman working at a beauty salon is given the chance to pursue her dream of becoming a model. Her pursuit of the goal is frowned upon by both her mother and her fiance, and threatened by rising social tensions as the country prepares for an election. Drama based on Shandana Minhas' novel Rafina, starring Amna Ilyas and Beo Raana Zafar. In Urdu and English(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Philadelphia Eagles as Super Bowl-winners(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter V, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost gives advice on how to prune overgrown evergreen shrubs and adds to his borders to create height. Rachel de Thame shows how to maintain containers and refresh with plants that will extend the display into autumn, and there's a feature on a gardener whose frequent absences from home have influenced the design and planting of his garden(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Carole Baxter looks at traditional lawn care and Brian Cunningham maintains his 'no-mow meadow'. Kirsty Wilson showcases indoor ideas to keep green fingers busy(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: Nigel focuses on dishes that bring people together, heading to Trowbridge in Wiltshire to discover what draws Moroccan families around a traditional lamb tagine. He also gets a lesson in the art of pairing spices for an Indonesian fish dish and meets Zoe, who shows him how to make Ghanaian jollof rice. Inspired by their recipes, Nigel creates a Sunday roast of pork belly and shredded duck with spiced flatbread(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Producers making baked goods fight it out for a place in the final. Phoebe, a youth worker from London, makes free-from chocolate brownies. Bavarian-born Angelika now lives in Lancashire where she makes and sells pretzels. Tech company worker Mike bakes traditional sourdough in his spare time. Finally, Utako works in finance in London, but makes Japanese bread on the side(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: Rick's journey draws to a close as he heads east from Oaxaca to the Yucatan Peninsula, where dishes like Papadzules and Sikil Pak are enjoyed in villages across the countryside. Here, the locals have lighter skins and blue-green eyes, and feast of the hottest chilli of them all, the Habanero. Rick ends his journey feasting on grilled seafood in Tulum, along the shores of the Caribbean Sea(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes journalist and TV presenter Angela Rippon back to Mount Edgcumbe Country Park in Cornwall, where she reminisces about the holiday she had there in 1952. The Strictly Come Dancing judge surprises her by arranging a trip on a vintage bus just like the one she would have taken to her accommodation at the time, which was a camp consisting of a series of converted Nissen huts that had been used by American soldiers during the war(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where Christina Trevanion and David Fletcher assess items including a large collection of boxed Corgi toys and some rare watches. Meanwhile, Paul Martin discovers the dramatic political life of one of Sandon's earlier owners, who was a member of William Pitt's cabinet in tumultuous times(n)

programme.title: Equestrian
programme.desc: Action from the competition in Stamford, Lincolnshire, a prestigious three-day eventing competition that forms part of the Rolex Grand Slam(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the half-marathon that ran from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields, as Mo Farah and Vivian Cheruiyot aimed to successfully defend their respective elite men's and women's titles, while tens of thousands of amateur runners raised money for charity(n)

programme.title: The Ganges with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Sue begins her final leg in the ancient city of Patna, which was once the centre of the British Empire's lucrative opium trade but is now known for education. In Kolkata, she is reunited with Rakhi, one of the street kids who made such an impression on the presenter during a previous visit two years earlier, then finds out what it takes to protect one of the world's most endangered cats - the Bengal tiger. Finally she reaches Saga Island in the mouth of the river, where from dusk till dawn two million people are expected to bathe in the holy river(n)

programme.title: Grizzly Bear Cubs and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. With only 10 weeks before they are due to be released, Gordon Buchanan begins training the cubs for their new life in the Russian wilderness. They learn survival skills and spend time exploring a special wilderness training zone just outside the enclosure where they can experience the wild. However, little cub Pasha is still struggling, and the team worry he may not make it(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The panel oversee more business propositions, with returning Dragon Theo Paphitis joining regulars Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies. Two entrepreneurs seeking funding for a range of drinks go to the lengths of setting up a bar in the studio, while other items up for assessment include a design for a pipe-fitting tool and a range of men's cosmetics. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the political influence of billionaire George Soros, who has been accused by conspiracy theorists and politicians alike of attempting to sway elections and control immigration policies. The programme questions how many of these stories have basis in fact, and how many are grounded in antisemitism(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: After reflecting on the fallout of the first week, Louise discovers something troubling about Tom. Rosamund Pike and Chris O'Dowd star in a comedy, written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas v Homophobia: The Legacy
programme.desc: The former rugby international continues his campaign to wipe out homophobia in football. With reported incidents of homophobic abuse at football matches at a record high, Gareth enlists the help of Parliament as he attempts to outlaw abuse in stadia. He also recruits Cardiff City, Leeds United and Norwich City in his campaign to tackle homophobia in the boardroom, the dressing room and on the terraces. All of this while coping with being the victim of a violent homophobic attack himself(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Suzy Klein welcomes the Shanghai Symphony Orchestra to the Royal Albert Hall for their Proms debut. With conductor Long Yu, they perform Qigang Chen's ‘The Five Elements', Mozart's Piano Concerto No 23 in A, with soloist and 2018 Leeds Piano Competition winner Eric Lu, and a triptych of Symphonic Dances by Rachmaninov(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Cerys Matthews introduces an evening of jazz, gospel and Broadway-style music from big-band giant Duke Ellington. The Nu Civilisation Orchestra, conducted by Peter Edwards, emulate Ellington's Sacred Concerts from the mid-60s, as the Proms premieres this brand-new version of the revered performances(n)

programme.title: Synth Britannia
programme.desc: An examination of the musical backdrop of the 1980s when synthpop was all the rage, having been popularised by bands including Depeche Mode, Ultravox and Soft Cell. The documentary also studies how this genre influenced the future of electronic music and groups such as Pet Shop Boys and New Order. Featuring interviews with Bernard Sumner, Gary Numan and Neil Tennant(n)

programme.title: Synth Britannia at the BBC
programme.desc: A showcase of 1970s and 80s synth music taken from the BBC archives. Popular acts in the genre include Roxy Music, New Order, Ultravox, Soft Cell, OMD, Yazoo and Depeche Mode(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: The team visits Bass Rock in the Firth of Forth, home to one of the world's largest gannet colonies. Tooni Mahto heads for St Andrews Bay to learn about communication between bottlenose dolphins, and Paul Rose meets Navy divers clearing unexploded bombs dropped by aircraft during training exercises at Cape Wrath. At the Sound of Mull, Frank Pope investigates efforts to preserve fragments of history locked away in some of the 25,000 wrecks that litter the waters around the British Isles(n)

programme.title: The Private Life of Pigs
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits a farm in Dartmoor, Devon, to explore the habits and intelligence of pigs. He assesses the sensitivity of their noses, which enables them to locate buried truffles, as well as make their way round a maze. Jimmy also finds out how piglets choose the right teat from which to feed and whether they can recognise themselves in a mirror(n)

programme.title: Darkness: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Jan and Louise make a gruesome discovery on a farm that Anders Kjeldsen stayed at as a child. Meanwhile, Emma and Julie plan to escape from the basement. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Darkness: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Louise realises that Anders Kjeldsen does not kill alone, but has a partner who is the dominant of the two, while Emma tries to persuade Stine to help her escape. In Danish(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: South Vietnamese forces fighting on their own in Laos suffer a terrible defeat, while a re-elected President Nixon announces Hanoi has agreed to a peace deal(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: The Watergate scandal forces President Nixon to resign, while North Vietnamese troops pour into the south and Saigon descends rapidly into chaos and collapses(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition first aired on June 2, featuring music by Aswad, Desireless, Tiffany, the Timelords, Voice of the Beehive, Five Star, Aztec Camera, Rod Stewart, Wet Wet Wet and Belinda Carlisle(n)

programme.title: Popular Voices at the BBC
programme.desc: From the BBC archives, a compilation of the enigmatic tones of some of popular music's most classic crooners. Featured are the wit and charm of Sammy Davis Jr, the honeyed voice of Nat King Cole, the swagger of Frank Sinatra, the swank of Bryan Ferry and the brooding of Willie Nelson.The programme also revisits some of the modernisers of crooning, from the theatrics of David Bowie to post-punk Echo and the Bunnymen, right up to the sensual murmurings of Lana Del Rey. Featuring clips from such programmes as Twiggs, The Vera Lynn Show, The Late Show, Top of the Pops and Whistle Test(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: The team visits Bass Rock in the Firth of Forth, home to one of the world's largest gannet colonies. programme.title: The Private Life of Pigs

Jimmy also finds out how piglets choose the right teat from which to feed and whether they can recognise themselves in a mirror(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: The BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra celebrates conductor Martyn Brabbins's 60th birthday in music by Vaughan Williams, Brahms and Elgar's Enigma Variations, from the Royal Albert Hall. Presented by Suzy Klein and Anna Meredith. Elgar's Enigma Variations is the inspiration for a new work commissioned from 14 living composers as a special birthday tribute to conductor Martyn Brabbins. Elgar's original set also features, as do Vaughan Williams's exquisite Serenade to Music and Brahms' Little Requiem, the Song of Destiny(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: An Anthem for England
programme.desc: Lovers of the hymn, including members of the Women's Institute and politicians, explain the appeal it holds for them, and give their opinions in the debate about whether Jerusalem should become England's official national anthem. programme.desc: Professor Chris Lintott visits the UK Astronomy Technology Centre in Edinburgh, to see a camera designed to take pictures of exoplanets, and journeys through exoplanet discoveries(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the achievements and discoveries of the Cassini space probe mission, which changed the way scientists regard the solar system. Its 13 years spent orbiting Saturn brought revelations of gigantic ice geysers, hidden underground oceans that could harbour life and a brand new moon coalescing in the planet's rings. Narrated by Paterson Joseph(n)

Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

Starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. Phil Davis, Niamh Cusack and Tom Georgeson guest star(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Memorial Van Damme. Highlights from the final Diamond League meeting of the year, with some of the world's best competing at the King Baudouin Stadium. This was the second part of the grand final, following last weekend's opener in Zurich. As well as the overall Diamond League champions being crowned, the winner of each event on the night would get a wildcard entry to the World Championships, which start at the end of this month. Among the stars expected to be in action were Great Britain's 100m gold medal hope in Doha, Dina Asher-Smith, American sprint sensation Noah Lyles in the 200m and reigning Olympic 110m hurdle champion Omar McLeod of Jamaica(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing

The line-up this year features former England footballers Alex Scott and David James, TV presenter Anneka Rice, actresses Catherine Tyldesley and Emma Barton, stand-up comedian Chris Ramsey, Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin, fashion model Emma Weymouth, Olympic gold medallist James Cracknell, Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing, CBBC presenter Karim Zeroual, singer Michelle Visage, BBC Breakfast sports correspondent Mike Bushell, YouTuber Saffron Barker and Paralympic table tennis player Will Bayley. Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood all return to the judging desk, joined by the newest member of the panel Motsi Mabuse, who replaces the outgoing Darcey Bussell. A home favourite also prevailed in the men's wheelchair race, with David Weir claiming his seventh title here, while Kenyan Vivian Cheruiyot won the elite women's event for the second time in three years and Poland's Martyna Snopek was the women's wheelchair champion(n)

programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: Solo climber Eric Jones embarks on one final mountain ascent at the age of 82 - journeying to the south-west ridge of the Torre Delago in the Italian Dolomites. Eric reflects on his own life and not just his climbing achievements, and explains how he fell in love with a young Australian teacher who abandoned her homeland for a new life in Snowdonia(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kelly Cates, Adrian Chiles, Andy Hamilton, Kate Williams, Mark Dolan, Rustie Lee, Esther Rantzen and Tim Vine(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition marking 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy waits by the water at Charlie's Yard to meet a dangerous adversary, but despite their past grievances the pair agree to a truce. Tommy receives a message from a mysterious stranger during his constituency surgery, while at his Westminster office, the MP makes Aberama a very tempting offer, and later invites Mosley to a party he is throwing for Lizzie's birthday. On the night of the event, things soon take a turn for the worse when a surprise guest arrives. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: In Herefordshire, Jules Hudson discovers why a good dose of country air might not be all that it's cracked up to be. John Craven enjoys the breathtaking north Cornwall coast, while Paul Martin is in the Brecon Beacons helping on a mission to save Britain's most endangered mammal - the water vole(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brothers help Elizabeth from Brighton to turn a paved-over blank square of land into a city garden full of colour and plants, on a healthy budget of £6,000(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich visit Mansfield to help Annette, who moved into her home with her three sons two years ago and is now ready to renovate the garden(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As a gunshot echoes round Albert Square, events inside the Vic go from bad to worse as someone's life hangs in the balance. Bianca tries to talk to Tiffany about everything that happened with Evie(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Four celebrities try to book their place in the semi-final, cooking a two-course meal to be judged by former champions Angellica Bell and Phil Vickery, and 2015 finalist Rylan Clark-Neil. With their last chance to impress, emotions in the kitchen are running high and the pressure proves too much for some contestants. The celebrity judges deliver some frank and cutting responses to some of the dishes, but others fare much better(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Derren Litten's comedy following a motley band of Scarborough residents who are bonded by family, friendship and their love of karaoke. It opens a week after a devastated Karen caught Mike drunkenly kissing another woman at the back of a seafront crab stall. It's only when they bump into each other at their regular Friday karaoke night that Mike finally has a chance to explain. But will Karen forgive him? Starring Jason Manford, Catherine Tyldesley and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites Kaiser Chiefs' frontman Ricky Wilson, and comedians Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons to express their anger over people and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include new towels, escalators and the bartender at the local pub(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Italy. John Inverdale presents action from both teams' final match before the World Cup, which came from St James' Park. With commentary by Andrew Cotter and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. Phil Davis, Niamh Cusack and Tom Georgeson guest star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Bird and Claire Thomson, and special guest Phyllis Logan. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary joins the crowds as she attends a music festival for the first time to explore the pop-up food stalls and enlists headliner Rick Astley to help make her speedy cheesy muffins. She also prepares scrumptious rosemary lamb steaks, vegetable kebabs and rum and raisin ice-cream(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including the Euro 2020 qualifiers taking place over the course of the week(n)

programme.title: Transplant Games
programme.desc: Alan Shearer tells the story of athletes whose lives were transformed by transplant surgery(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage from Stockton-on-Tees, as elite athletes compete in both track and field events. Double Paralympic gold medallist Jonnie Peacock is set to take part in the to take part in the IPC 100m, and heptathlon star Katarina Johnson-Thompson plans to double up in the long jump and 150m(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Memorial Van Damme. Highlights from the final Diamond League meeting of the year, with some of the world's best competing at the King Baudouin Stadium. This was the second part of the grand final, following last weekend's opener in Zurich. As well as the overall Diamond League champions being crowned, the winner of each event on the night would get a wildcard entry to the World Championships, which start at the end of this month. Among the stars expected to be in action were Great Britain's 100m gold medal hope in Doha, Dina Asher-Smith, American sprint sensation Noah Lyles in the 200m and reigning Olympic 110m hurdle champion Omar McLeod of Jamaica(n)

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: Game show in which two pairs of complete strangers team up, each battling to outdo the other by listing as much as they know about a given subject. The first couple to win two rounds get to play for £50,000 and each time the champions defeat a new set of opponents their winnings mount up(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, the contestants are Rick Edwards, Michael Brooks, Mark Thompson, Maggie Aderin- Pocock, Heather Couper, Nigel Henbest, Dallas Campbell and Lucie Green(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The pro-celebrity ballroom contest hosted by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman returns with a bang, as the Queen of Pop herself Kylie Minogue performs a medley of her biggest hits alongside a dazzling routine from the professionals. The line-up this year features former England footballers Alex Scott and David James, TV presenter Anneka Rice, actresses Catherine Tyldesley and Emma Barton, stand-up comedian Chris Ramsey, Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin, fashion model Emma Weymouth, Olympic gold medallist James Cracknell, Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing, CBBC presenter Karim Zeroual, singer Michelle Visage, BBC Breakfast sports correspondent Mike Bushell, YouTuber Saffron Barker and Paralympic table tennis player Will Bayley. Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood all return to the judging desk, joined by the newest member of the panel Motsi Mabuse, who replaces the outgoing Darcey Bussell. The show also sees the celebs take to the floor for the very first time in a spectacular group d(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Archie takes matters into her own hands to try and get justice for a wronged patient, and decides to help him escape from the emergency department before the police can catch him. Already making progress with his mental health after his suicide attempt, Iain runs into an old friend, Dixie, and their chance meeting encourages Iain to think about his potential and his future. Meanwhile, Rash struggles to cope under an increasing amount of pressure, and David finds it difficult living with his teenage son(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as a doting mother-of-six who constantly interferes in her children's lives. Agnes tries to solve her son Dermot's relationship problems by giving his girlfriend advice, and Grandad ends up in hospital after Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes awry(n)

programme.title: Sliding Doors (1998)
programme.desc: A London woman's entire future hinges on whether or not she catches a train. In one version of events, she gets the chance to start afresh and lead a happy new life, but in the other she is stuck supporting her lazy, unfaithful boyfriend. Comedy drama, presenting two parallel stories featuring the same character. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, John Hannah and John Lynch(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: The seven remaining contestants are tested on their lyric writing and their flow, as they are challenged to perform over a host of beats with different BPMs, from afrobeat to drum 'n' bass. Then, the artists are tasked with penning and performing a heartfelt dedication to a loved one(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's opening game of the season, Chicago Bears v Green Bay Packers at Soldier Field(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Victoria Fritz present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage of the world's biggest half-marathon, which sees tens of thousands of runners of all abilities negotiate the course from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields. British favourite Mo Farah won the men's elite race for a record fifth time in 2018, beating New Zealand's Jake Robertson into second place as he did 12 months earlier. A home favourite also prevailed in the men's wheelchair race, with David Weir claiming his seventh title here, while Kenyan Vivian Cheruiyot won the elite women's event for the second time in three years and Poland's Martyna Snopek was the women's wheelchair champion(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores sacred architecture 350 years after Christopher Wren started work on St Paul's Cathedral. Josie d'Arby meets Christian artists collective Morphe Arts(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an active couple return to their roots in Shropshire, looking for a property within their £350,000 budget that gives them the great outdoors on their doorstep(n)
