programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: The pro-celebrity ballroom contest hosted by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman returns with a bang, as the Queen of Pop herself Kylie Minogue performs a medley of her biggest hits alongside a dazzling routine from the professionals. The line-up this year features former England footballers Alex Scott and David James, TV presenter Anneka Rice, actresses Catherine Tyldesley and Emma Barton, stand-up comedian Chris Ramsey, Radio 1 DJ Dev Griffin, fashion model Emma Weymouth, Olympic gold medallist James Cracknell, Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing, CBBC presenter Karim Zeroual, singer Michelle Visage, BBC Breakfast sports correspondent Mike Bushell, YouTuber Saffron Barker and Paralympic table tennis player Will Bayley. Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood all return to the judging desk, joined by the newest member of the panel Motsi Mabuse, who replaces the outgoing Darcey Bussell. The show also sees the celebs take to the floor for the very first time in a spectacular group d(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Archie takes matters into her own hands to try and get justice for a wronged patient, and decides to help him escape from the emergency department before the police can catch him. Already making progress with his mental health after his suicide attempt, Iain runs into an old friend, Dixie, and their chance meeting encourages Iain to think about his potential and his future. Meanwhile, Rash struggles to cope under an increasing amount of pressure, and David finds it difficult living with his teenage son(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as a doting mother-of-six who constantly interferes in her children's lives. Agnes tries to solve her son Dermot's relationship problems by giving his girlfriend advice, and Grandad ends up in hospital after Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes awry(n)

programme.title: Sliding Doors (1998)
programme.desc: A London woman's entire future hinges on whether or not she catches a train. In one version of events, she gets the chance to start afresh and lead a happy new life, but in the other she is stuck supporting her lazy, unfaithful boyfriend. Comedy drama, presenting two parallel stories featuring the same character. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, John Hannah and John Lynch(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: The seven remaining contestants are tested on their lyric writing and their flow, as they are challenged to perform over a host of beats with different BPMs, from afrobeat to drum 'n' bass. Then, the artists are tasked with penning and performing a heartfelt dedication to a loved one(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's opening game of the season, Chicago Bears v Green Bay Packers at Soldier Field(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Victoria Fritz present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage of the world's biggest half-marathon, which sees tens of thousands of runners of all abilities negotiate the course from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields. British favourite Mo Farah won the men's elite race for a record fifth time in 2018, beating New Zealand's Jake Robertson into second place as he did 12 months earlier. A home favourite also prevailed in the men's wheelchair race, with David Weir claiming his seventh title here, while Kenyan Vivian Cheruiyot won the elite women's event for the second time in three years and Poland's Martyna Snopek was the women's wheelchair champion(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores sacred architecture 350 years after Christopher Wren started work on St Paul's Cathedral. Josie d'Arby meets Christian artists collective Morphe Arts(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an active couple return to their roots in Shropshire, looking for a property within their £350,000 budget that gives them the great outdoors on their doorstep(n)

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore reclaims three items from a Walsall recycling centre, with designer Rupert Blanchard given the task of bringing a boring bookcase into the 21st century(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kelly Cates, Adrian Chiles, Andy Hamilton, Kate Williams, Mark Dolan, Rustie Lee, Esther Rantzen and Tim Vine(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition marking 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy waits by the water at Charlie's Yard to meet a dangerous adversary, but despite their past grievances the pair agree to a truce. Tommy receives a message from a mysterious stranger during his constituency surgery, while at his Westminster office, the MP makes Aberama a very tempting offer, and later invites Mosley to a party he is throwing for Lizzie's birthday. On the night of the event, things soon take a turn for the worse when a surprise guest arrives. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Spring arrives, and the youngster is very fond of the new lambs(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports(n)

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Local postman Henry McSparrow finds a long-lost love letter and sets out on a quest to deliver it to the addressee - it may have been a missive of love, but has that affection since waned?(n)

programme.title: Alba
programme.desc: Stories that hit the headlines in 1974, including the three-day week, the progress of the SNP and Glasgow's rubbish problem. Plus, a look at the year's chart hits such as Pick Up the Pieces by Average White Band, Autobahn by Kraftwerk and This Town Ain't Big Enough by Sparks(n)

programme.title: Togaidh Sinn Fonn (Join in the Music)
programme.desc: Showcasing Gaelic music and song, featuring performances by harp duo Sìleas and Mairi Morrison(n)

programme.title: Dreach Ùr
programme.desc: Shona Masson challenges Shona MacLellan in Stornoway to transform an old jacket into a stylish top. She also makes some decorative flowers from a fabric sample book and some cushions from old jumpers(n)

programme.title: On the Tracks of the Wild Otter
programme.desc: An insight into the lives of wild otters in Shetland, filmed by wildlife cameraman Hugh Miles and originally aired in 1983 as part of BBC Two's The World About Us series(n)

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Dosca play the Oblique Set at Na Trads 2015(n)

programme.title: The Flatey Enigma
programme.desc: Icelandic drama set in 1971, following a professor of Nordic Studies who is accused of murder. To prove her innocence, she must solve a riddle in a medieval manuscript about Norse kings, the Flatey Enigma. On top of that, Johanna also has to face her deepest fear - the man she ran away from 10 years ago. Starring Lara Johanna Jonsdottir. In Icelandic(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Tthe challenges of winter on the croft as Sweeny contends with 2018's infamous Beast from the East and some really cold weather. As a full time crofter, Sweeny is also constantly exploring new ideas to ensure a steady income throughout the year. One of these new ideas leads him to a new hobby - knitting - as he heads to the Isle of Skye to get creative with his wool(n)

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: Seven years in the planning, two years in its construction, the Harris Distillery is in its first year of existence. This first of three episodes follows the lead up to and first working year at the Harris Distillery. After some delays in construction and the inevitable technical glitches, their Harris Gin goes straight into production. Hot on its heels is the distillery's primary function, and the team works up to distilling its first Hearach whisky. The distillery staff have all been recruited locally and are collectively known as the 'Tarbert Ten'. They share a commonality in having never been involved in the distillery business, but are keen to prove that they are more than able to create a historic happening on the Isle of Harris(n)

programme.title: Clann Pheter Roraidh
programme.desc: The story of the Campbell family from the Isle of Barra. Tony Kearney is a well-known Scottish actor and broadcaster, but his family's talents extend back generations. The programme features the children of Peter Rory, Tony's late grandfather, as cameras witness how their creative legacy lives on(n)

programme.title: The Flatey Enigma
programme.desc: Icelandic drama set in 1971, following a professor of Nordic Studies who is accused of murder. To prove her innocence, she must solve a riddle in a medieval manuscript about Norse kings, the Flatey Enigma. On top of that, Johanna also has to face her deepest fear - the man she ran away from 10 years ago. Starring Lara Johanna Jonsdottir. In Icelandic(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen

programme.title: Save Money: Good Health
programme.desc: Sian Williams takes a look at products aimed at fighting gum disease, while Ranj Singh answers viewers' questions on common concerns regarding children's health, from head lice to chicken pox. Plus, two members of the public try out different treatments for dandruff and the weekly guide to vitamin and mineral supplements continues with calcium(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle experiences the world beneath the Yorkshire Dales as he explores Britain's biggest underground cavern - Gaping Gill. Liz Bonnin visits Scotland's Forest Park of Galloway where she tracks one of our rarest mammals the pine martin. Plus, at home in the Cotswolds, actress Angela Griffin shares her love of local fresh produce with nine-year-old daughter Missey(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 4.15 Sprint Cup Stakes from Haydock Park, which follows races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.35. Plus, races from 2.05 and 3.15 at Kempton Park, and from 2.45 and 3.55 at Ascot. With analysis by Jason Weaver, reports from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary by Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Bulgaria (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the Group A qualifier at Wembley Stadium, as Gareth Southgate's men look to maintain their 100 per cent record in qualifying so far. These countries last met during the qualification campaign for Euro 2012, with the Three Lions winning comfortably on both occasions. The game on this ground in September 2010 finished 4-0 to the home side, with Jermain Defoe bagging a hat-trick for Fabio Capello's team. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Ian Wright, Lee Dixon and Roy Keane, reports by Gabriel Clarke, and commentary from Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions

The second installment features the return of Lost Voice Guy, the first comedian to ever win the show, the group Mersey Girls from series 11, and series 5's winner Jai McDowall. Joining the British entries is an unusual act from Germany - Alexa Lauenburger and her eight performing dogs(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Bulgaria. Mark Pougatch presents action from the Group A qualifier, which took place at Wembley Stadium. Plus, action from the rest of today's key fixtures, including France v Albania and Serbia v Portugal. With analysis from Glenn Hoddle and Lee Dixon(n)

John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh prepares a surprise for Rob and Margaret Isdale, a Grantham couple in their seventies who have fostered more than 150 children over the past 45 years. Alan draws inspiration from Frances Hodgson Burnett's novel The Secret Garden, creating ancient looking follies and winding paths, and even turning a normal garden shed into a mock-Tudor feature(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the spin-off from the talent show in which winners and memorable acts from past series return to the fray, competing against each other and performers from Got Talent shows from around the world. Joining the British entries is an unusual act from Germany - Alexa Lauenburger and her eight performing dogs(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Comedian Tim Vine, co-host of The Great British Bake Off Mel Giedroyc and former Pussycat Doll Ashley Roberts line up to 'see it and say it'. Competing to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues and win money for charity, one of them receives the chance to bag £50,000 for a good cause. Stephen Mulhern hosts(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites Strictly Come Dancing head judge Shirley Ballas, Mrs Brown's Boys star Danny O'Carroll and Corrie's Andy Whyment to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity(n)

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: A pair of exes and a father-and-daughter duo take part in the game show, competing to answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages, with a potential jackpot of more than £50,000 up for grabs. Hosted by Phillip Schofield(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Charlotte sets out to avoid Sidney, until a serious accident forces them together and to reappraise each other. When Tom brings a new doctor to the town, he and Lady Denham clash, while Clara makes a bold move in her bid to outplay Sir Edward and Esther. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Charlotte Spencer, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: The comedian embarks on a journey across the US, following the migratory trail of the Scots through America - starting in New York and finishing in Nashville, Tennessee. The first episode sees Billy open the New York Tartan Day parade in front of a 30,000-strong crowd. He then heads to Plymouth, Massachusetts, where the pilgrims first landed on American soil, followed by a meeting with the Native American tribe who almost had their people and language wiped out(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: Head of Security Margo is determined to get to the bottom of a argument which started over some boiled eggs, and the incident inspires one of the inmates involved to write a poem. Meanwhile, prisoner Adie is tasked with the unpleasant job of cleaning up a 'dirty protest', where a prisoner has used his own excrement to write on the walls(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Sian O'Callaghan goes missing after a Friday night out in Swindon, Detective Superintendent Stephen Fulcher knows that he and his team must move fast to have any chance of finding her alive. While Sian's family desperately wait for news, the police search for any clues as to where she could be. Fact-based crime drama, starring Martin Freeman, Imelda Staunton and Siobhan Finneran(n)

The game on this ground in September 2010 finished 4-0 to the home side, with Jermain Defoe bagging a hat-trick for Fabio Capello's team. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mr Carson and Mrs Hughes' wedding is fast approaching, but a misunderstanding threatens to derail the occasion, and when the big day arrives it also brings a few unexpected guests. Meanwhile, Edith reconnects with an old acquaintance, who comes to her aid in her hour of need, and over at the Dower House, Spratt has something to hide, meaning Denker is hot on his trail. Daisy jumps to conclusions about one of the countess's plots, and tensions mount over the hospital takeover, prompting Isobel to offend a friend. Period drama, starring Jim Carter, Phyllis Logan, Elizabeth McGovern and Laura Carmichael(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica tackles a case in Osaka, Japan, where a motorcyclist romancing the daughter of a traditionally minded family finds his life on the line. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: The History of Mr Polly
programme.desc: Comedian Lee Evans stars in this adaptation of HG Wells' novel about an ordinary man who turns his back on a failing business and loveless marriage to pursue his dreams. Driven to despair by the aimlessness of life in the drapery trade, unfriendly neighbours and the incessant nagging of his wife, Alfred Polly decides to end it all(n)

programme.title: Carry On at Your Convenience (1971)
programme.desc: Production at toilet manufacturers WC Boggs is brought to a halt when an over-eager trade union representative calls a strike. However, worker unrest is quickly quelled by a jolly trip to the seaside. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Cope, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Bernard Bresslaw(n)

programme.title: Great Estates of Scotland
programme.desc: Dumfries House is one of Britain's most beautiful stately homes, boasting sumptuous interiors and magnificent furnishings. Built between 1754 and 1759 by the 5th Earl of Dumfries, and with a unique collection of Chippendale furniture, the house has been described as an 18th-century time capsule, as the principal rooms and their contents have remained unchanged for over 250 years. In June 2007, the Prince of Wales headed a consortium of charities and heritage bodies to purchase this unique house, its contents and adjoining land. This is the story of his efforts to keep this historical jewel intact and accessible to the public(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is lumbered with the job of running the depot canteen, and makes the mistake of appointing Olive to do the cooking. With her in charge of the kitchen, disaster figures prominently on the menu. Vintage Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are challenged to a darts match by canny clippies Iris and Jenny - who compensate for a lack of skill by using their feminine charms to gain an unfair advantage(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby wines and dines Miss Jones in a suit surreptitiously 'borrowed' from stroppy wrestler Spooner - who turns up halfway through the main course intent on exacting revenge. Vintage comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The first episode of the whimsical rural drama based on HE Bates' novels. Tax collector Cedric Charlton visits the Larkin household intent on getting the head of the family - Pop - to correctly fill out his tax forms. In so doing he falls for attractive daughter Mariette. Starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Taxman Charley decides to escape from the office and go strawberry-picking with Mariette and the rest of the Larkin family - a move that leaves his career hanging in the balance. Light-hearted drama based on the novels by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ma can hardly contain her enthusiasm now that Mariette and Charley have announced their intention to marry. Unfortunately, Pop puts all his finances into bidding for a stately home, casting a shadow over her extravagant plans for the wedding. Rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse is assigned to protect an informant who is undergoing emergency surgery. The case brings back unpleasant memories for the detective, while his colleagues are thrown into disarray when Bright is also rushed to hospital. Morse becomes fixated with a series of mysterious deaths that have occurred on the ward, and tries to find the scientific explanation behind stories of a curse(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A student is attacked on campus, and Jack takes time off from questioning two robbery suspects to catch the thug responsible before he can strike again. The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lorraine's social worker turns up, Mark asks Annie to bring up his wages at the farm meeting, and Neil pays Kim a surprise visit(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lorraine tells her shocking story to Carol, who cannot believe she never knew what her husband was doing to her(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim slips off to Harrogate to do some shopping without Frank, Eric and Elizabeth are finding it difficult to adapt to living together, and Annie plans to sell her farm shares(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Sister Brigid faces a struggle as she tries to convince her superior at the convent to seek medical help when it becomes obvious she is seriously ill. Jill discovers the answer to a puzzling case, before receiving news from Samantha about the father of her baby. Frankie tells Lizzie his amorous plans, then finds them thwarted when he sees Staff Nurse Taylor with Dr Goodwin. Linda Armstrong, Amy Robbins, Scott Taylor and Michelle Hardwick star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop can barely contain his glee as his beloved Mariette's wedding draws closer, until a summons from the magistrate threatens to turn it into a far from 'perfick' day. Whimsical drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dispirited by a wet English summer, the Larkins cross the Channel in the hope that the French way of life will have a beneficial effect on the family. David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: Joanna begins a 2,000-mile journey across Japan in Hokkaido, where she meets one of the most important animals in Japanese culture, the red-crowned crane. She arrives in Sapporo during the middle of the annual Snow Festival and meets members of the local indigenous community, before travelling into the Fukushima exclusion zone and taking a bullet train from Nagano to Tokyo(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: In the wake of the fall of France to the Germans, guests flood into the hotel, including one who catches Lady Hamilton's eye - the Comte de St Claire. Peggy worries about Billy's enthusiasm to sign up as soon as he turns 18, but knows there is little she can do to protect him when his mind looks like it's made up to join his father's regiment, infamously used as canon fodder. When Max faces hostility from the staff, Emma goes out on a limb to defend him - and immediately regrets it(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Joe is offered the opportunity of a lifetime, but finds himself unexpectedly torn as the moral responsibility of staying in England to tell the story of the war weighs heavily on his shoulders. Betsey's mother Gloria comes for a surprise stay, leaving Sonny unsettled and wary of the reaction she incites from Betsey, while Lucian and Lady Hamilton continue their flirtation and Emma anxiously awaits news of Freddie when she hears that a plane from Squadron 392 has gone down(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is pleased to have the twins stay with her, but does not want them to take sides. Percy suggests Emily drops her protest, and Denise is pleased when Hanif takes her out(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike realises Doug must have stolen the Jag, Tracy celebrates sitting her last exam, and Emily writes to the Gazette about the wheel clampers(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Merton is suspended following allegations of theft made by a woman he was dating, but Mike believes he is innocent and sets about clearing his name. Meanwhile, Vernon becomes concerned about Bernie's strange behaviour. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Jason Durr, Jan Francis and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is called to testify in a court case concerning a crooked investment firm, but the key witness dies shortly afterwards. Angela Lansbury stars as the sleuth, with Lisa Eichhorn and Larry Linville(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: One of the firm's clippies gets evicted leaving Stan feeling guilty as he was late getting her home, so he persuades Mum to take her in as a lodger. However, he soon regrets making the offer after she arrives accompanied by a large dog. Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney with Patricia Clapton(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan's plans for a romantic day at home are thwarted by Arthur's ramshackle motorbike, which unexpectedly breaks down on him. Comedy, with Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby tries to drum up support for his local election candidate, but finds Alan and Philip have other ideas about who should represent their interests. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack has another go at Mark for not working hard on the farm, Lynn encourages Lorraine to do something with her life, and Elizabeth tells Eric about her meeting with Michael(n)

programme.desc: Elizabeth suggests to Eric that they start a joint account, Frank sees Kim and Neil together, and Kathy suspects Kim is having an affair(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg is convinced he is going to be fired, Ken is horrified when Deirdre tells him Tracy is not returning to school, and Emily is puzzled by Percy's attitude(n)

programme.desc: Emily's story appears in the Gazette, the Duckworths worry that Alf will ask them to repay him all they owe, and the CID warn Mike that they suspect him of fraud(n)

programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: Joanna begins a 2,000-mile journey across Japan in Hokkaido, where she meets one of the most important animals in Japanese culture, the red-crowned crane. She arrives in Sapporo during the middle of the annual Snow Festival and meets members of the local indigenous community, before travelling into the Fukushima exclusion zone and taking a bullet train from Nagano to Tokyo(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: In the wake of the fall of France to the Germans, guests flood into the hotel, including one who catches Lady Hamilton's eye - the Comte de St Claire. Peggy worries about Billy's enthusiasm to sign up as soon as he turns 18, but knows there is little she can do to protect him when his mind looks like it's made up to join his father's regiment, infamously used as canon fodder. When Max faces hostility from the staff, Emma goes out on a limb to defend him - and immediately regrets it(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Following Norman's complaints about the standard of hospital food, Figgis leads the patients on a hunger strike. Comedy, starring Christopher Strauli and James Bolam(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Stewart, Donalee, Daz, and Rosie pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A well-meaning but hot-headed cop (Sylvester Stallone) reacts too quickly to a violent incident and ends up unwittingly shooting a boy - becoming a public hate figure as a result(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A missing small-time crook turns up dead, leading to a murder investigation - but Carter's girlfriend develops a peculiar interest in the proceedings. Geraldine James guest stars, with Dennis Waterman and John Thaw(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is repaid a debt in dodgy £20 notes, but by the time it comes to light they are counterfeit his creditor is heading on a flight to India. Once the Winchester is pinpointed as the source of the forged currency, Arthur also finds he has Chisholm breathing down his neck. Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide(n)

programme.title: Cycling: Vuelta a Espana
programme.desc: Action from the 12th stage of the Grand Tour race, which took the riders on a 171.4km course from Circuito de Navarra to Bilbao. Gary Imlach presents, with reports from Daniel Friebe and commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle spring into action when East German agents blackmail a government employee into revealing secret plans for a new type of jet-engine tank. Featuring an appearance by playwright Lynda La Plante, alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen(n)

programme.desc: Theo makes a discovery at a murder scene that makes him realise his nephew is on drugs. He turns to a former addict for support in putting the young man through cold turkey(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner fears a genetically engineered virus may be on the loose following the death of a hospital patient and those who came into contact with her(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a CIA agent dies after being thrown off a balcony, the unit sets up a fake arms deal with the German terrorist cell responsible. However, the gunrunners uncover the ploy, kidnap Bodie and transform him into a walking time-bomb. Lewis Collins stars(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A crook arranges the conversion of replica firearms into the real thing and sells them on to small-time hoodlums. When one of the arms misfires killing both a policeman and its holder, Regan is intent on discovering the source before more innocent lives are lost. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Cycling: Vuelta a Espana
programme.desc: Action from the 13th stage of the Grand Tour race - a 166.4km course from Bilbao to Los Machucos. Gary Imlach presents, with reports from Daniel Friebe and commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel to Dorset with England international angler Matt Godfrey, where they target a 20lb carp on an overnight challenge, before trying their hand at sea fishing for wrasse at Portland Bill, and finally trying to catch a 5lb chub at Throop Fishery(n)

programme.title: The World Is Not Enough (1999)
programme.desc: James Bond is assigned to protect the heiress of an oil dynasty after her father is killed in a bombing at MI6 headquarters. The attack is the work of an infamous terrorist who is unable to feel pain after surviving a gunshot to his head, and who now intends to destroy the world's supply of oil. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Robert Carlyle, Sophie Marceau and Robbie Coltrane(n)

Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How malt vinegar is brewed, how Aga stoves are made to last a lifetime and a chance to see the work taking place at Britain's last remaining tennis ball factory(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Short Fuse (1972)
programme.desc: The son of a chemical company's founder is blackmailed by his uncle, who wants to force him into selling the business. The cunning heir responds by murdering his relative with a box of exploding cigars, but finds himself facing the dishevelled detective, who is convinced the resulting fatal detonation was no accident. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Roddy McDowall and Ida Lupino(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the Canada-US border lies a vast lake that hides a dark history. For Native Americans it's an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade plunges into the heart of a waterfall in the Pacific Northwest on a mission to catch the oldest river monster of his career(n)

programme.title: Silverstone Classic
programme.desc: Tiff Needell and Louise Goodman present highlights of the 29th edition of the motor racing festival, which was held over three days at the famous British motorsport circuit. Among the anniversaries honoured were Jackie Stewart's maiden win at Silverstone in 1969, 100 years of Bentley and a half-century of the Ford Capri(n)

programme.title: World of Sport: TV Firsts
programme.desc: Richard Henwood looks back at television sporting firsts, including footage of a 14-year-old Ronnie O'Sullivan who would go on to take snooker by storm(n)

programme.title: Cycling: Vuelta a Espana

programme.desc: Scientists and historians use isotope and DNA analysis to identify the crew of Henry VIII's flagship, which sank off the coast of Portsmouth in 1545. With the help of Mary Rose Trust curator Alex Hildred, the programme uncovers evidence that many of the sailors were of African origin, and traces surviving descendants of the only two members of the crew who can be identified by name. programme.title: Treasure Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: Twelve people are left on an uninhabited island in the Pacific, marooned and facing the toughest five weeks of their lives, with cash prizes worth £100,000 waiting to be discovered. What the participants do with the money when they find it is up to them, but if they leave early, they leave with nothing. In just the clothes on their back, with a day's supply of water and a few basic tools, the group is abandoned on the treacherous south east side of the island. They are immediately confronted with their first big decision when a helicopter flies overhead and parachutes a box of cash on the north shore. Do they chase the money or try to make camp? Bear keeps a watchful eye on proceedings(n)

In the first edition, Jamali travels to the US, where he meets a new breed of porn star known as cammers, who perform directly for their clients via their laptops. programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Following the convictions of serial killer Stephen Port and murderer Gerald Matovu, who both used GHB as their weapon, Dispatches has conducted an eight-month investigation with BuzzFeed News into the use of the drug among gay men. With the largest ever survey of GHB users, Dispatches reveals for the first time the human cost of the epidemic scale of abuse, with over a quarter of users reporting being sexually assaulted, almost half having overdosed, thousands of hospital admissions, and a hidden crime spree: rape, murder, and the live-streaming of sexual abuse. The law allows GBL to be sold for cleaning purposes, which enables easy access to industrial quantities of it online, which can then be used/sold as GHB to users. Dispatches interviews men who have been deliberately overdosed, raped, and those including Lord Brian Paddick who have lost partners and family members(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor takes a rail journey around the globe, he begins by visiting France, Germany and Hungary before heading towards Turkey, which straddles Asia and Europe. In Paris, he meets up with a friend who explains the finer points of French social etiquette, while in Germany, Tony compares a gentle spin in a replica of the first automobile with an exhilarating blast in the latest Formula 1 car. He then travels across India and Burma, beginning in Delhi, where he visits one of the city's oldest markets, before taking a ride on the Himalayan Mountain Railway(n)

programme.title: Mysteries of the Bermuda Triangle
programme.desc: Documentary about the area of the north Atlantic between Florida, Puerto Rico and Bermuda where it is alleged many boats and aeroplanes have gone missing. Stories about strange events in the region include a US Coast Guard ship that detected a phantom landmass on its radar, two 'ghost rockets' that flew silently over a US Navy ship and Russian submarines that reported all manner of bizarre activity. There are interviews with some of the leading experts on this mystery, air traffic controllers Bob Greene and Bob Gish, pilots Bruce Gernon and Fred Grieser, underwater explorers Evan Kovacs and Les Savege and weather expert Dr Stephen Leatherman(n)

programme.title: The Missing Evidence
programme.desc: The identity of one of history's most notorious serial killers has generated many theories over the years since the three-month period in 1888 when a number of women were murdered on the streets of Whitechapel. Journalist Christer Holmgren believes that he has found a suspect who can not only be linked directly to one of the murders, but also whose daily routine could be consistent with all the other deaths. Having studied the case for more than three decades, his focus eventually fell on a witness called Charles Cross, whose real name turned out to be Charles Allen Lechmere, and he found a mass of incriminating evidence(n)

programme.title: Red Arrows: Kings of the Sky

programme.title: Cricket on 5
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from the fifth and final day of the Fourth Test in the five-match series, held at Emirates Old Trafford. The series was finely poised at 1-1 following England's stunning victory at Headingley in the previous contest, but a win for the Aussies in this match would have meant they could not lose the urn they claimed on home soil in the 2017/18 season(n)

programme.title: Inside British Airways
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at the airline, charting the fortunes of the business and following the people who work to keep the planes flying and passengers happy. In the first edition, a team in Seattle take delivery of the airline's 30th Boeing 787 Dreamliner, while menu design manager Mark Tazziolli faces the passengers' verdicts as his new meals are served on a flight(n)

programme.title: World's Most Luxurious...
programme.desc: Only the world's richest can possibly afford a super yacht, but it is not the price tag that makes these extravagant vessels exceptional, it is the luxurious details. Here, members of the billionaire Haidar family display their 52-metre floating palace, where a 13-strong staff attends to their every need. Plus, cameras sneak a peek behind the scenes as a charter yacht is prepared for a group of wealthy Americans and follow Becki Milton, private yacht shopper to the super-rich, as she finds the ultimate getaway for three secretive celebs(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Julian Clary narrates a compilation of disastrous mishaps from comedy shows. Featuring a stunt fight that ended badly for comedian Russell Howard, an incident that left Tony Hancock determined to never perform live again, and a rare outtake from Tom, Dick and Harriet. That long-forgotten sitcom from 1982 and 1983 featured former Return of the Saint star Ian Ogilvy and actor/director Lionel Jeffries. Their stunt involving a partly submerged car could have ended in disaster(n)

programme.title: In Harm's Way (1965)

They get another chance to prove their worth when they are placed in charge of a vital counter-offensive. Second World War drama, starring John Wayne, Kirk Douglas and Henry Fonda(n)

programme.title: The Poseidon Adventure (1972)
programme.desc: A luxury cruise liner at sea on New Year's Eve is struck by a freak tidal wave, which leaves it capsized and several passengers and crew trapped. Disaster movie, starring Gene Hackman, Ernest Borgnine, Shelley Winters, Leslie Nielsen and Roddy McDowall(n)

Starring Kenneth More, Dana Wynter, Laurence Naismith and Karel Stepanek(n)

They attempt to turn their fortunes around and redeem their reputation by competing in the World Championships of A Cappella in Copenhagen. Comedy sequel, starring Anna Kendrick and Rebel Wilson(n)

programme.title: The Green Mile (1999)
programme.desc: A convicted child-killer of towering height and massive build is condemned to death row, but astonishes the guards with his good nature and miraculous healing powers. They believe the gentle giant is incapable of crime, and emotions run high as they realise there is no hope of overturning his death sentence. Supernatural drama, based on a novel by Stephen King, starring Tom Hanks, Michael Clarke Duncan, David Morse and Harry Dean Stanton(n)

programme.title: Horns (2013)
programme.desc: A man who has become an outcast after being accused of murdering his own girlfriend wakes up one morning to find he has grown horns. He discovers he now has the power to compel people to confess their sins or act on their darkest desires, and uses his supernatural gifts to expose the real killer. Horror based on Joe Hill's novel, starring Daniel Radcliffe and Juno Temple(n)

programme.title: Beneath the Planet of the Apes (1969)
programme.desc: A second astronaut arrives on the strange simian-dominated world searching for his predecessor. As he journeys into the infamous forbidden zone, he discovers an underground cult of psychic mutants. Meanwhile, a military coup has taken place among the ape society, leading to an inevitable battle. Sci-fi sequel, with James Franciscus, Charlton Heston and Kim Hunter(n)

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora! (1970)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama recounting the bombing of Pearl Harbour from both the Japanese and American perspectives, reconstructing the events leading up to the devastating attack on the US naval base in 1941. Starring Martin Balsam, So Yamamura, Joseph Cotten, James Whitmore and Jason Robards(n)

programme.title: The Rewrite (2014)
programme.desc: A washed-up Hollywood screenwriter takes a job at a small university, planning to exploit his position as a local celebrity and do as little work as possible. His cynical outlook begins to change when he falls for a single mother working toward her degree, while a talented pupil makes him consider what he wants from his life. Romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Marisa Tomei, Allison Janney and JK Simmons(n)

programme.title: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire (2013)
programme.desc: Katniss Everdeen's victory in a televised death match makes her a focus for revolution against the totalitarian nation she lives in. Sci-fi adventure sequel based on the second book in Suzanne Collins' trilogy of novels, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Liam Hemsworth, Donald Sutherland and Philip Seymour Hoffman(n)

Sci-fi thriller based on Stephen King's novel, starring John Cusack and Samuel L Jackson(n)

They hope ballroom dancing will entertain the guests, some of whom object to the pink flock wallpaper and knitted toilet roll holders. Nick Lawler seems to be warming to the owners, but one of them suspects his change of heart is all part of a cunning plan(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nick Lawler and business partner Kate Wilson host the competition at the Winder Hall Country House B&B in the Lake District, but a clash of egos ensues when they try to lecture the other participants about how to manage their hospitality establishments, and a disastrous breakfast service leaves contestants unimpressed(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Sheffield architects Howard Evans and Prue Chiles set out to transform Mandy's 1980s redbrick house, which stands out like a sore thumb among the grand Victorian properties it sits alongside on Nottingham's prestigious Park Estate. The duo are familiar with unlocking the potential of buildings, but this could be their biggest challenge yet(n)

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell host the grand final of the painting contest. The three remaining artists paint actress Laura Linney at the National Portrait Gallery, before the winner of the career-changing £10,000 commission is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The keepers try to work out why male red panda Jung is losing weight, with a full medical examination scheduled after a special diet fails to solve the problem. West African mandrill JC is being challenged for his dominant position by his sons Ludo and Jareth and, while JC is taken for some health checks, Ludo gets 'over-friendly' with his dad's girlfriend. Tempers start to fray among Chester Zoo's Humboldt penguin colony during the annual moult - can youngsters Frazzle and Munch avoid being picked on by the grown-ups?(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: David, Gill and their son Freddie hope Noel Fitzpatrick can help their Labrador Scooby, who has a life-threatening tumour in his leg. The vet goes to extraordinary lengths to save the life of Tiger the cat after his heart stops during his recovery from an operation, and when a lurcher called Otis brought in with shotgun wounds, Noel must extract the bullet before working out how to save the animal's leg. Plus, will a pair of tights help a French bulldog walk again?(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Donna McDougal's search for the armour in the lake continues. Tim and Krys, with the help of Tim's brother Ben, continue in their struggle to get the attic converted in time for the first guests of the season. Dick and his mother-in-law Jenny struggle with preparing and freezing 227kg of pork, before attempting to turn the final 2kg into salamis(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: Amina Mansour, a young woman of Egyptian heritage, is found badly injured in the hammam spa where she works after being attacked with a knife. Amina has an Italian boyfriend and dresses in Western clothes, and it occurs to Valeria that this may have caused tension with her family, whose way of life is still anchored in tradition. In Italian with English subtitles(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A young man who has had a severe reaction to a recreational drug is wheeled into resus by paramedics, and senior sister Jen and the medical team face a challenge to calm their patient down so they can treat him. A 65-year-old woman is brought in by her best friends after hitting her head when she fell off a chair while playing bingo, and a 31-year-old man with multiple sclerosis is rushed in after developing a fever at home(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Schizophrenic Graham, 75, is brought to King's College Hospital after being stabbed in the neck by a stranger on his doorstep, and needs a CT scan to reveal the extent of his wound. Former nursery nurse Pat, 89, also arrives on the ward, having been found at the bottom of the stairs at home with no recollection of how she got there, while palliative care nurse Tracey treats 72-year-old retired postman Michael, who was recently diagnosed with lung cancer(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: David, Gill and their son Freddie hope Noel Fitzpatrick can help their Labrador Scooby, who has a life-threatening tumour in his leg. The vet goes to extraordinary lengths to save the life of Tiger the cat after his heart stops during his recovery from an operation, and when a lurcher called Otis brought in with shotgun wounds, Noel must extract the bullet before working out how to save the animal's leg. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty finds out that shellfish consumed by millions have been feeding on plastic discarded in the sea, with shocking global implications. Kate Quilton finds out whether the makers of Marmite can really predict whether people will love or hate their distinctive yeast-based spread, as claimed in their latest TV advert, and Tebbutt aims to solve the age-old mystery of how to smoothly extract ketchup from a bottle - by running across a pond of custard(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the last day of the competition and there is tension in the air, as Cholmeley Arms hosts John and Lesley want to get to the bottom of Peter's comments(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

The day gets off to a tricky start when she attempts to make home-made pastry for the first time, but her nan saves the day. The guests are suitably impressed by her home, and the dancing entertainment goes down even better than the food(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: The crews are stretched to their limits as they deal with a 19-year-old man who has had a motorbike crash, and an elderly woman showing signs of a stroke, but refusing treatment(n)

programme.title: 9/11: The Falling Man
programme.desc: Documentary about the people who jumped from the World Trade Centre during the September 11 terrorist attacks, and one man in particular whose plight was one of the defining images of the tragedy. Contributors include the photographer who captured the shot, which was published in newspapers before being banned, and the journalist who claimed to have identified the falling man(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: On a busy Saturday night at King's College Hospital, staff have to juggle the needs of elderly patients and sick children alongside those who are drunk or violent. Paramedics bring 12-year-old Grace into resus after she falls down a flight of stairs, while Sarah and Andy are concerned for their 10-month-old son Dexter, who has a high temperature and is unresponsive. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Meanwhile, Geraldine, 60, has been injured while trying to cross the road, and after recently being released from prison 40-year-old Brian is in minors with a broken ankle, but the swelling means his electronic curfew tag will have to be removed(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a race from London's Wembley Stadium to Milan's San Siro, the home of Italian football giants AC and Inter. A price restriction leads Jeremy to grab the keys to a Shelby Mustang GT500, which costs the equivalent of £35,000 in America and packs a supercharged punch of more than 650bhp. James and Richard pin their hopes on the pan-European rail network in a bid to secure the prize of tickets to a top-flight football match, with nothing but a long journey home awaiting the losers. On the test track, Jeremy puts Toyota's GT86 coupe through its paces, and its promise of entertaining rear-wheel-drive handling should put a smile on his face. Plus, singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald gets to grips with the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorset's Jurassic coast to explore the county's hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on a 45-mile hike through the countryside of the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the area's rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps him relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. From there, he travels up what used to be secret paths to Hever Castle and finds out how the monarch's reign brought not only huge social, political and industrial change, but also fame and disaster to the women who caught his eye He ends this leg of his journey in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes on a four-day trek through the Kintail region of the north-western Highlands, examining the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s along the way. He starts by visiting Iron Age dwellings in Shiel Bridge, and heads to the rebuilt castle on Eilean Donan, the original of which was destroyed after invading Spanish troops landed there and were attacked by British warships. Tony ends in Glen Shiel, the site of a climactic battle in 1719 where royalist troops defeated the rebels(n)

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: The remains of three U-boats discovered in the Bristol Channel. Their exact identity and how they came to be there is not known, so divers explore the wrecks and with the help of British and German experts reveal the mystery submarines. Series investigating deep-sea wrecks off the south-west coast of England(n)

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: The fate of a Canadian warship that went to the aid of an American freighter rocked by an explosion in 1944. More than 60 years on, divers uncover new evidence of the sinking(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the vast and diverse continent, aiming to show its landscapes and wildlife as they have never been seen before. He begins in the Kalahari desert, Africa's south-west corner, where clever meerkats are outsmarted by a wily bird's use of mimicry, a solitary rhino is seen to exhibit previously unknown sociable behaviours, terrifying insects prey on baby birds and the rarest fish in the world is filmed for the first time. In the neighbouring Namib desert, which receives almost no rain, the competition for scant resources can be fierce, as proven by a ferocious fight for supremacy between two giraffes(n)

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Now all their children have flown the nest, Vanessa and car fanatic Gary want to start the next phase of their lives by setting up a new home. The problem is Vanessa wants to settle in Lowestoft with family and friends nearby, while Gary wants to be in southern Spain where he can be close to Marbella's car scene. Help is at hand from Laura Hamilton, who delivers three homes in Lowestoft for their maximum £195,000 UK budget, while Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three amazing properties in the hills above Marbella with their £178,000 limit(n)

programme.title: The Great Hotel Escape
programme.desc: Steph and Dom Parker give their verdict on Sally and Robin's new B&B in Shropshire, while in Yorkshire, Mark manages the first guests at his bed and breakfast by himself(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Steph is trapped with multiple injuries after a bad smash near Alnwick in Northumberland, while an accident with a bonfire leaves Paul with serious burns. Meanwhile, in Thetford Forest in Norfolk, the medics have difficulty reaching horse-rider Alice, who has badly broken her ankle(n)

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at Devon and Cornwall Police's struggle to meet the needs of the communities they serve when reoffending is high, and there are cuts to social and mental health services. Force incident manager Adele faces difficult decisions in protecting the lives of the public as she deals with the most serious and life-threatening cases that come into the Devon and Cornwall police control room. She has to determine whether the use of firearms should be authorised to tackle a 15-year-old in possession of a sword, and if there is time to wait 30 minutes for a negotiator to talk a woman away from the edge of a cliff(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Maitre-d Fred Sirieix returns to the luxury hotel, where he and his matchmakers welcome singletons from across the UK. First to nab a poolside lounger is royal family-obsessed perfumer Perry, who's already turning heads in some natty gold trunks. He's on the scent for a man who can get the seal of approval from his beloved mum and dog Dolly. His match is 43-year-old art dealer Naveed, whose pre-date nerves could be his undoing. Single dad and builder Richard is trying to find someone he can resonate with on a spiritual level. Heartbroken after the breakup of his marriage, he's checked in hoping to restore his faith in love(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host the second round of the culinary competition, which this time focuses on biscuits. For the signature round, the contestants must concoct a luxurious chocolate-coated bake. The technical challenge is a nostalgic one for Paul Hollywood, who asks the contestants to prepare a snack that was a favourite of his father's. Finally, the showstopper round requires the bakers to rise to an eye-catching challenge that is designed to test which of them will snap under the pressure(n)

programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.desc: The team must make an important decision concerning the safety of a chimp group when alpha male Hananya continues to exhibit violent behaviour. programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The park hosts a morning sing-off, and golden-cheeked gibbon Zoey introduces a new member of the family. Over in Hananya's group, hot potatoes are on the menu, and a fight among the female capuchins leaves Phoebe with a bite wound that requires surgery(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Baby woolly monkey Paulo meets his new family for the first time, while chimpanzee Eddi has fun with his young brother Bart. Pregnant orang-utan Amy gets to grips with a breast pump, and emotions run high when a marmoset arrives at the park(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist visits the jungles of Borneo to meet creatures that like to glide through life - the harlequin tree frog and the little-known colugo - often referred to, incorrectly, as a flying lemur. programme.title: I Was There: The Great War Interviews
programme.desc: In the early 1960s, the BBC spoke to almost 300 people about their experiences during the First World War for landmark series The Great War, but only a fraction of the interviews were broadcast. This programme airs a selection of the original material, most of which has never been screened before, with recollections of the horrors of artillery bombardment and fleeting outbreaks of peace on the battlefield, as well as the stories of those on the home front waiting for news of their loved ones. Narrated by Samuel West(n)

programme.title: The Statue (1971)
programme.desc: A linguist's wife sculpts a nude statue of him to celebrate his achievements. However, when she grows weary of his neglect, she reveals that its impressive physique was modelled on another man. Convinced she has taken a lover, he sets out to identify the man behind the sculpture. Comedy, starring David Niven, Virna Lisi and Robert Vaughn(n)

programme.title: What's Cooking? (2000)
programme.desc: The members of four diverse families meet up in their respective homes to celebrate Thanksgiving, where the growing Americanisation of the younger generation becomes ever more apparent to their parents, and tangled emotional battles are played out. programme.title: Stephen Fry's Key to the City
programme.desc: The QI host tours the City of London, discovering the hidden mysteries of this rich and powerful square mile. Along the way, he visits the Bank of England's vaults, witnesses high drama at the London Metal Exchange as dealers buy and sell stocks, and experiences Dead Man's Walk at the Old Bailey, where many condemned criminals trod their final steps. Plus, as a recipient of the Freedom of the City of London, Stephen finds out just what privileges this gives him(n)

programme.title: Battle of Britain: The Real Story
programme.desc: James Holland explores the Battle of Britain from the German point of view. The author focuses on the tactics, technology and intelligence available to both nations, and examines how they decided to use their resources. He also gains access to first-hand testimonies from German pilots, and diaries and documents that reveal a previously unknown side to this confrontation(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars(n)

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: The English Teacher (2013)
programme.desc: A teacher is reunited with her favourite past pupil, who has returned from New York having failed to succeed as a playwright and under pressure from his family to get a job. She puts her reputation on the line to prove her protege has talent by getting the school to put on a production of his play. Comedy drama, starring Julianne Moore, Michael Angarano and Greg Kinnear(n)
